executives.name: Denise Paulonis

executives.relevance: CEO articulating the exact problem Algolia NeuralSearch solves — curation and guided discovery across 18,500+ SKUs

executives.name: Denise Paulonis

intelligence_signals.text: BuiltWith and job postings confirm Salesforce Commerce Cloud as the e-commerce platform. Algolia has a native SFCC cartridge — no custom integration required.

findings.title: Zero NLP / Semantic Understanding
findings.tested_query: best purple shampoo for blonde hair
findings.observation: Query returns keyword-matched results only. Words like 'blonde,' 'best,' and attribute intent ('purple shampoo') are treated as separate tokens, not semantic signals. Natural language product discovery fails completely for Sally's 1,300+ hair color SKUs.
findings.impact_stat: 72% of e-commerce sites completely fail search expectations
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch — vector + keyword hybrid understands attribute queries as semantic signals, not token matches
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% conversion rate, +150% sales contribution from search, -88% bounce rate
findings.expected_behavior: Purple shampoo products filtered for blonde hair type, ranked by attribute relevance.
findings.actual_behavior: Keyword match only — 'best' and 'blonde' ignored. No semantic understanding of user intent.

findings.title: No Federated Search — Content Commerce Gap
findings.observation: Searching 'return policy' returns product results, not help content or FAQ documentation. CosmoProf Institute education content (balayage tutorials, color formulation guides) is not discoverable through the product search experience.
findings.impact_stat: 34% of users attempted to search for non-product content on e-commerce sites
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Multi-Index Federated Search — products, education content, FAQ, and pro-only inventory unified in one results pane
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 150% increase in order rate for new users (Algolia Recommend)
findings.expected_behavior: Help page or FAQ content about returns + product results in a unified view.
findings.actual_behavior: Products only (likely 'return' keyword matches). No federated content across help pages.

findings.title: No Personalization Layer
findings.observation: A licensed colorist and a first-time DIY shopper searching 'shampoo' see identical results. No segment-based (pro vs. DIY) or individual-user reranking. CQuotient present but limited to rule-based targeting, not real-time user affinity.
findings.impact_stat: 48% of consumers spend more with personalized experiences
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Personalization — real-time user affinity profiles re-rank search results by segment, browse history, and brand affinity
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +50% conversion rate after enabling Algolia Personalization, +36% click-through rate
findings.expected_behavior: Personalized results: color-safe shampoos ranked higher based on prior hair color browsing.
findings.actual_behavior: Zero personalization visible. Same generic results for every visitor. No 'Recommended for you.'

findings.title: Weak Recommendations on PDP
findings.observation: Product detail page shows limited or no 'Frequently Bought Together' or 'Similar Items.' For a retailer selling complementary products (color + developer + gloves + applicator brush), missing FBT recommendations leaves basket revenue on the table. No dedicated recommendation engine detected in BuiltWith.
findings.impact_stat: Recommendations drive up to 31% of e-commerce revenue
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend — FBT, Similar Items, and Trending models on PDP and post-add-to-cart
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 150% increase in order rate for new users during Black Friday, 32% increase in add-to-cart rate
findings.expected_behavior: 'Frequently Bought Together': developer, gloves, applicator brush. 'Similar Items' carousel.
findings.actual_behavior: Limited or no recommendations visible. No FBT module. Massive cross-sell opportunity missed.

findings.title: Static Filters — Poor Intent Detection
findings.observation: Searching 'flat iron' returns results with only static 'Pickup & Delivery' and 'Brand' filters. No temperature settings, plate width, or wattage filters — the most relevant attributes for this product category. Static filters appear identical across all search contexts regardless of query intent.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of e-commerce sites have critical issues with search query type handling
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Dynamic Faceting — automatically surfaces most relevant filter attributes by query context
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% conversion rate improvement, -88% bounce rate reduction
findings.expected_behavior: Dynamic filters: 'Temperature settings' for flat iron, 'Heat protection level', 'Plate width'.
findings.actual_behavior: Static filters (Pickup & Delivery, Brand only) across all searches. No context-aware faceting.

findings.title: Typo Tolerance Gap (Moderate)
findings.observation: Basic single-character typos handled ('shampo' → shampoo results). More complex transpositions ('condtioner') need verification. No visible 'Did You Mean?' correction. Native SFCC typo handling is keyword-level only, without ML-backed fuzzy matching.
findings.impact_stat: 81% of U.S. consumers leave after unsuccessful search
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Typo Tolerance — configurable single/double typo handling, transposition matching, and 'Did You Mean?' UI component
findings.algolia_case_study_result: -88% bounce rate reduction after Algolia implementation
findings.expected_behavior: Results for 'shampoo' despite misspelling, with visible 'Did you mean?' correction.
findings.actual_behavior: Basic single-character typos handled. Complex transpositions may fail. No 'Did You Mean?' shown.

findings.title: SAYT Present (Positive)
findings.observation: Autocomplete dropdown appears with product suggestions. Good foundation to layer Algolia Query Suggestions (trending + recent) and NeuralSearch on top of existing SAYT.
findings.impact_stat: 24% sales and conversion boost from autocomplete
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions + InstantSearch — enriches SAYT with trending, recent, and AI-powered suggestions
findings.expected_behavior: Search-as-you-type dropdown with relevant product suggestions.
findings.actual_behavior: SAYT present — autocomplete dropdown appears with product and query suggestions.

findings.title: Brand Intent Recognition (Positive)
findings.observation: Brand-level intent ('CHI') returns branded products correctly. Good baseline for Algolia Query Rules overlay (brand page redirects, bannering, boosted brand content).
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Rules — extend brand recognition with dedicated brand landing pages, banners, and priority boosts
findings.expected_behavior: Brand intent recognized — CHI products returned for brand query.
findings.actual_behavior: Brand recognition works well. CHI products appear correctly for brand search.

gap_pairs.quote: a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO explicitly identifies the search efficiency gap. Audit confirms: native SFCC Einstein Search fails semantic/NLP queries — 'best purple shampoo for blonde hair' returns keyword matches only.

gap_pairs.quote: I'm overwhelmed by choices. TikTok offers me choices. Everybody in the world offers me choices. Help me make the right choice.
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO articulates the core customer pain point. Audit confirms: search results are identical for all users regardless of segment or history — no 'choice curation' at the search layer.

gap_pairs.quote: Customers acquired through LCOD spend two times more than customers acquired through other means over their first year with us.
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO proves guided discovery drives 2x AOV. Audit confirms: PDP has no 'Frequently Bought Together' or similar recommendations — the digital equivalent of guided discovery is missing.

gap_pairs.quote: Global ecommerce sales increased 11% to $111 million and represented 12% of total net sales.
gap_pairs.finding_title: No Federated Search — Content Commerce Gap
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CFO confirms e-commerce scale ($111M/quarter). Audit confirms: that scale has no federated search — help content, education, and pro-only inventory are invisible to search users.

golden_angle.headline: The First-Mover Window Is Open in Professional Beauty
golden_angle.body: No major professional beauty retailer uses Algolia. Sephora (the prestige competitor) is on Constructor.io. Ulta (the mass-market competitor) uses nothing AI-powered. If Sally acts now — especially in the BSG platform overhaul window — they become Algolia's beauty lighthouse customer and leapfrog both competitors on search experience.

ae_fields.entry_point: Chris Hansen, VP Digital Product — most active on LinkedIn, speaks at CommerceNext, promoting digital team roles
ae_fields.opening_line: I've been reading about Sally Ignited — it sounds like a comprehensive transformation. I noticed you're calling out AI, personalization, and seamless customer journey as priorities. Search touches all three of those...
ae_fields.timing_urgency: BSG platform update is in progress Spring 2026 — the window to include search technology before the stack is locked is narrow
ae_fields.objection_platform_lock: Algolia has a native SFCC cartridge. It's plug-and-play, not rip-and-replace.
ae_fields.objection_budget: We understand Fuel for Growth means every dollar must prove ROI. Algolia reduces merchandising labor AND lifts revenue — net positive. $5.47M/year at 10% lift.
ae_fields.ask: 30-minute NeuralSearch demo on Sally's live catalog
ae_fields.urgency_driver: Sephora is already on AI search. The longer Sally waits, the wider the experience gap.
ae_fields.next_step_action: Reach out to Chris Hansen (VP Digital Product) via LinkedIn — lead with Sally Ignited AI priority and the Einstein Search limitation against modern NLP queries. Reference the BSG platform update timing as the urgent window.
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: I've been reading about Sally Ignited — it sounds like a comprehensive transformation. I noticed you're calling out AI, personalization, and seamless customer journey as priorities. Search touches all three of those, and right now you're running native SFCC Einstein Search, which doesn't do NLP or semantic understanding.
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: Can I show you a 20-minute NeuralSearch demo on Sally's live catalog? I'll run the same queries your customers type — 'natural hair color treatment', 'toner for brassy hair at home' — and show you the difference between what Einstein Search returns today versus what Algolia NeuralSearch returns. No pitch, just the contrast.

strategic_angles.evidence: Sally Ignited strategy explicitly calls out AI, personalization, and seamless customer journey. Search is the intersection of all three.
Sephora uses Constructor.io for AI-powered search. Algolia NeuralSearch matches or exceeds Constructor capabilities with a native SFCC cartridge.
Sally serves DIY consumers AND licensed professionals. CosmoProf Institute expansion creates a unique federated search opportunity (products + education).
BSG app and e-commerce platform update planned Spring 2026 — includes AI and personalization. Perfect timing to include search upgrade before stack is locked.

icp_mapping_anchor_lines: Sally Beauty told analysts their 'Sally Ignited' strategy calls out AI, personalization, and seamless customer journey — site search is the only touchpoint that touches all three simultaneously.

Denise Paulonis said she wants customers to feel like Sally Beauty has helped them 'make the right choice' — but native SFCC Einstein Search can't handle 'natural hair color treatment' as a natural language query.
Sephora uses Constructor.io for AI search. No major professional beauty retailer uses Algolia today — Sally could be our lighthouse customer for this vertical.
Sally's BSG platform overhaul is in progress Spring 2026 — the window to include search technology before the stack is locked is narrow. Retrofit later costs 3x more.
At 3.0-3.5% conversion and $62 AOV, even a 10% search improvement on $54.7M addressable revenue = $5.47M/year — that math clears the Fuel for Growth investment bar.

touches.subject: LinkedIn connection — Sally Ignited + search
touches.body: Chris — I've been reading about Sally Ignited. You're calling out AI, personalization, and seamless customer journey as priorities. Search touches all three, and right now sallybeauty.com is on native SFCC Einstein Search without NLP. Sephora moved to Constructor.io. Would you be open to connecting?

touches.subject: NLP gap: what Sally customers are searching vs. what they get
touches.body: Chris — I ran Sally's catalog against NeuralSearch: 'natural hair color treatment' — Einstein returns keyword matches only; NeuralSearch understands the intent and surfaces semantically relevant results. 'Help me make the right choice' is your CEO's stated goal. NLP search is how you get there. Worth 20 minutes?

touches.subject: SFCC Algolia cartridge — plug-and-play, not rip-and-replace
touches.body: Joe — Algolia has a native SFCC cartridge. It's not a re-platforming project — it drops in alongside your existing SFCC stack. BSG platform overhaul in Spring 2026 is the perfect timing to include search rather than retrofit it later. Happy to share the architecture.

touches.subject: BSG federated search: products + CosmoProf education in one query
touches.body: Chris — when a licensed pro searches 'balayage' on BSG, should they see products AND CosmoProf Institute education content in the same results? Federated search is a single Algolia API call. Right now those are two separate siloed experiences. The BSG overhaul is the right moment to fix this.

touches.subject: Sally Beauty first-mover advantage in AI search
touches.body: Denise — no major professional beauty retailer uses Algolia today. Ulta doesn't. Sephora uses Constructor.io. Sally could be our lighthouse customer for this vertical — we'd invest heavily in making it a reference case. At $5.47M/year in estimated search lift (10% scenario), the ROI clears the Fuel for Growth investment bar.

touches.subject: Closing the loop — audit and demo available
touches.body: Chris — I've shared 4 specific observations on Sally's search opportunity over the past 3 weeks. If the BSG platform decisions are already locked, I understand. If search is still in scope for the overhaul, there's a 20-minute demo ready. The full audit report is yours regardless.
